intro: Brevo exposes a free tier while Lob starts at $260/mo. Both tools position themselves for transactional emails (receipts, alerts), so the decision rarely comes down to surface features. In practice, Brevo — Marketing and transactional email/SMS platform with automation, CRM, and a free tier API. Lob — Messaging API (Email / SMS / Voice) tool for developers. Specializes in Direct Mail API. Brevo carries low lock-in (Marketing/email, SMTP portable) while Lob is medium (Direct mail API). Brevo rates 5/5 on transparency versus 4/5 for Lob. Both tools list transactional emails (receipts, alerts) as a headline strength, which is why pricing and lock-in usually decide the call. Both tools surface overlapping when-to-use scenarios, including transactional emails (receipts, alerts). The honest trade-off: Brevo is not built for internal projects, and Lob is not the right call for internal projects, so neither is a universal default. Transparency scores land at 5/5 for Brevo and 4/5 for Lob, which reflects how openly each tool documents pricing, limits, and terms.
quick_take: Brevo is for transactional emails (receipts, alerts); Lob overlaps on the same job; decide based on pricing model.
verdict_a: Choose Brevo if transactional emails (receipts, alerts) and you need a free tier to validate fit.
verdict_b: Choose Lob if transactional emails (receipts, alerts) and the $260/mo entry price fits your budget.

faq.a: Brevo has a free tier and Lob starts at $260/mo, so at the lowest end Brevo is effectively cheaper. Once you exceed the free limits, the comparison depends on per-unit rates not published here. Price against your actual send or usage volume.

faq.q: Can I migrate from Brevo to Lob?
faq.a: Technically you can move between any two tools in the same category, but effort varies: leaving Brevo is relatively light thanks to its low lock-in, and onboarding to Lob involves medium lock-in (Direct mail API). Plan for data export, re-integration, and a parallel-run window rather than a hard cutover.

faq.q: Which has better developer experience, Brevo or Lob?
faq.a: Both Brevo and Lob score 4/5 on developer experience in our data, so there's no clear winner on that axis alone. The real differentiator usually lands in SDK fit for your language, error handling, and docs depth. A half-day integration spike tells you more than the score.

faq.q: Is Lob a good alternative to Brevo?
faq.a: Yes, Lob targets the same job Brevo does — transactional emails (receipts, alerts) — so functionally it slots in as an alternative. Pricing model (subscription from $260/mo vs freemium with a free tier) and lock-in (medium lock-in (Direct mail API) vs low lock-in (Marketing/email, SMTP portable)) usually decide which is the better fit. Validate on your actual workload. Note: Lob is explicitly not for internal projects.
